'''Tibannucks''' were intelligent creatures native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. There, they fed on the [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|gas]], which was then processed by their four [[Lung/Legends|lungs]]. The Tibannucks were [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] and [[Death/Legends|killed]] when they found themselves in the core of [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operations of [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], a floating [[City/Legends|settlement]]; in particular the [[Tibannuck queen mother|queen mother]] was a target with a considerable [[Bounty/Legends|price]] on her head.

==Biology and appearance==
Tibannucks appeared to be primitive and inane, but their outward appearance belied their intelligence. They had four [[Lung/Legends|lungs]], which were separated into two sections each—the first took in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|gasses]] they ingested, while the second section spread them throughout the body. The Tibannuck head featured a [[Nose/Legends|snout]], [[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and a purple patch at the crown. Each member of the [[Species/Legends|species]] also had a tail, as well as two [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] that ended in long, webbed digits. The webbing could be extended or contracted, depending on the movement of the fingers. Tibannucks were capable of flight, and they were red-skinned. Their long necks were marked on either side with large knob-like protrusions, and featured a green tuft at the base of the head. The Tibannuck's limbs were marked with purple and red tufts, and ended with long, brown digits that could be splayed into membranous wings. Toward the tail, the Tibannuck featured two blue and green protrusions on either side of the body. Their tails were not unlike their necks, insofar as they also featured a series of knob-like protrusions on either side.<ref name="daa" />

==Behavior==
The Tibbanucks were native to the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], where they fed on rare [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] gas.<ref name="daa" />

==History==
While dwelling on Bespin, the Tibannucks sometimes found themselves in the middle of [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operations run from the major floating [[City/Legends|settlement]], [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. As such, they were often [[Hunting/Legends|caught]] and [[Death/Legends|killed]]. The [[Tibannuck queen mother]] was a particularly desirable target, and a 2,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]] [[Bounty/Legends|reward]] was offered in the event of her death.<ref name="daa" /> However, [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] ended the bounties on tibannuck kills, but the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] refused to recognize those creatures as [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] despite evidences from a [[sentientologist]]. They were a menace for [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] during the [[Cloud City Grand Prix/Legends|Cloud City Grand Prix]] as the race crossed a region that was part of the feeding grounds of the tibannucks.<ref name="The Jewel of Yavin">''[[The Jewel of Yavin]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[Nathaniel Gabor]] created the Tibannuck species through [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine|''Star Wars Galaxy'']] magazine's "[[Design An Alien]]" competition. Gabor, aged fourteen at the time, submitted his entry after the competition was launched in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 3]] ([[1995]]). When the winners were published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] ([[1996]]), Gabor's entry came third in the "Under 16 years old" division, after the entries for [[Jeigh Esse]] and the [[Critokian]]. Continuity editor [[Leland Chee]] later stated that the published "Design an Alien" material was to be considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM}}</ref>
